_comment: Merge the hooks block below into ~/.claude/settings.json. Replace YOUR_DISCORD_USER_ID with your numeric Discord user ID (or remove ' YOUR_DISCORD_USER_ID' from a hook to post without pinging). The activity hooks need no user ID (that feed never pings). Requires bash, curl, and jq on the machine. The Notification matcher whitelists the notification_type values that mean Claude needs you: tool/permission prompts (which also cover AskUserQuestion and plan-mode), MCP elicitation dialogs, and idle_prompt (the ~60s 'waiting for your input' signal for when Claude is finished/stuck and it's your turn). The ping feed (notify-discord) is intentionally NOT on Stop (turn-end is not a needs-input signal); for hard-blocks-only, remove idle_prompt from its matcher. The usage tracker (notify-discord-usage) IS wired to PostToolUse (all tools) + Stop -- it posts only when official usage crosses a new whole percent (5h or weekly), silent except at milestones (25/50/80/90/100%) where it @-mentions you; it reads its webhook from ~/.claude/discord_usage_webhook.txt.
